input.command: cd /workspaces/agno && python -c "from agents.sleep_tools import sleep_toolkit; print(sleep_toolkit.name)"

input.new_string:
from agents.sleep_tools import sleep_toolkit
from agents.validity_attester_agent import VALIDITY_ATTESTER_AGENT_ID, build_validity_attester_agent

input.old_string:
def test_build_leader_includes_extra_notify_tools(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    team = build_project_team(
        **_build_kwargs(
            monkeypatch,
            settings=_settings(),
            prompts=_PROMPTS,
            leader_extra_tools=[notify_error],
        ),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )

    assert team is not None
    team_tools = team.tools or []
    assert len(team_tools) == 3
    assert team_tools[2].name == "notifyError"
input.new_string:
def test_build_leader_includes_extra_notify_tools(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    team = build_project_team(
        **_build_kwargs(
            monkeypatch,
            settings=_settings(),
            prompts=_PROMPTS,
            leader_extra_tools=[notify_error],
        ),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )

    assert team is not None
    team_tools = team.tools or []
    assert len(team_tools) == 3
    assert team_tools[2].name == "notifyError"


def test_build_leader_includes_sleep_tool(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    team = build_project_team(
        **_build_kwargs(
            monkeypatch,
            settings=_settings(),
            prompts=_PROMPTS,
            leader_extra_tools=[sleep_toolkit],
        ),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )

    assert team is not None
    team_tools = team.tools or []
    assert len(team_tools) == 3
    assert team_tools[2] is sleep_toolkit

input.description: Run new sleep tool unit test

content.text:
Added the sleep toolkit to Project Team’s leader tools via `leader_extra_tools` in `app/main.py`, alongside `notify_error` and Telegram tools. The Project Team leader coordinates runs in coordinate mode, so that’s where shared utilities like sleep belong.

```187:187:app/main.py
    leader_extra_tools=[notify_error, build_telegram_tools(), sleep_toolkit],
```

`sleep_toolkit` was already imported in `main.py` for the tool registry; no new wiring was needed beyond passing it into `build_project_team`.

A unit test confirms the toolkit is attached when passed through `leader_extra_tools`.

[REDACTED]

<user_query>
now add this tool to the Github agent we have
</user_query>

Added `sleep_toolkit` to the GitHub agent, matching the repo explorer agent:

```46:47:agents/github_agent.py
        tools=[github_mcp_tools(), sleep_toolkit],
```

The agent is built in `app/main.py` via `_github_agents()`, so it will pick up the change on the next deploy without further wiring.
